📐
amplifier-bundle-dot-graph
Knowledge · Validation · Rendering · Intelligence
First-class DOT/Graphviz infrastructure for the Amplifier ecosystem
Active · Open Source
The Insight
“The diagram didn’t document the bug.
Drawing the diagram found the bug.”

When you force an agent to build a valid graph representation of a system, every node must exist, every edge must connect. The ambiguity that prose hides gets exposed.

This surfaces bugs, dead code, and structural gaps that LSP, compilers, and traditional static analysis miss — because those tools check syntax, not architecture.

What It Is
One include line. Complete DOT capabilities.
includes: - bundle: dot-graph:behaviors/dot-graph

🤖 2 Expert Agents

A DOT author that creates and refines diagrams, and an independent reviewer that validates quality.

📚 5 Skills

Syntax reference, pattern library, analysis methodology, quality standards, and graph intelligence.

🔧 4 Tool Operations

Validate, render, analyze, and setup — all through a single unified tool interface.

⚡ Zero Required Dependencies

Knowledge layer works immediately. Validation and rendering degrade gracefully without graphviz.

Use Case 1
Reconciliation Forcing Function

Building a valid graph forces structural commitment. Agents can’t produce a valid digraph with contradictory edges or nodes that don’t connect.

What prose hides

“The auth module connects to the database” — which auth module? Which database? Through what interface? Prose tolerates this ambiguity.

What graphs expose

auth_service -> db_pool -> postgres_primary — every node declared, every edge connected. No hiding behind vagueness.

The reconciliation process itself is the investigation — it surfaces bugs, dead code, duplicate paths, and gaps that no other approach catches.

Use Case 1 — Demo
Reconciliation in action
BEFORE — What we believed
AFTER — What the graph revealed: 7 issues surfaced
Use Case 2
Dense Context Representation
10×
more token-efficient
than prose

A 200-line DOT file encodes what would take 2,000+ lines of prose to describe. Nodes, edges, clusters, attributes — all structured and parseable.

In LLM-based workflows where the context window is the fundamental constraint, this compression matters.

// 6 lines = complete subsystem subgraph cluster_auth { label="Auth Service" login -> validate -> token_issue token_issue -> session_store validate -> rate_limiter }
Use Case 3
Multi-Scale Navigation

Subgraphs as zoom levels. Google Maps for codebases. The context window is the fixed “screen size.”

🗺️

Zoom Out

System overview — major clusters, data flows, entry points.

🔍

Zoom In

Cluster detail — individual nodes, internal edges, interfaces.

🔬

Inspect

Node detail — attributes, metadata, all connections in and out.

Use Case 3 — Demo
Multi-scale in practice
ZOOM OUT — System overview
ZOOM IN — Auth subsystem detail (22 nodes)
Use Case 4
Analysis Substrate

Parse DOT into graph objects via NetworkX for cheap, code-based intelligence. Zero LLM tokens. Millisecond execution.

stats
reachability
unreachable
cycles
paths
critical_path
subgraph_extract
diff
The Analysis-to-Artifact Flywheel
Agent produces DOT
→
Analyze finds issues
→
Annotated DOT
→
Render visual
→
Act on findings
→
🔄 Loop
Use Case 5
Multi-Modal Bridge

DOT renders to SVG, PNG, PDF — visual artifacts for users, dashboards, documentation, and vision-capable models. One source file, three audiences.

📝

Text-Based Agents

Read DOT directly as structured text. Parse nodes, edges, attributes. Full semantic understanding.

👁️

Visual Consumers

Rendered SVG/PNG for humans, dashboards, and docs. Architecture visible at a glance.

⚙️

Programmatic Tools

Parse graph structure for analysis, diffing, CI/CD validation. Machine-readable by design.

# One source, multiple outputs dot_render(format="svg") # visual dot_render(format="png") # image dot_render(format="json") # data
Use Case 6
Workflow & Recipe Visualization

Complex multi-step workflows become immediately comprehensible as visual flow graphs. Stages, gates, loops, conditional routing — all visible at a glance.

Use Case 7
Investigation Artifacts

Forces agents to commit to specific structural claims during discovery work. No vague “the system has some coupling issues.”

🔄 Cycles

“These 4 nodes form a cycle” — named, specific, verifiable.

🎯 Bottlenecks

“This node has 12 incoming edges” — a concrete single point of failure.

🔌 Gaps

“3 clusters, zero cross-connections” — a measurable integration gap.

The graph structure is the evidence. Every claim is verifiable by inspecting the DOT source.

What’s Inside
Two Expert Agents
✏️

dot-author

model_role: coding

Creates, refines, and explains DOT diagrams. Full syntax knowledge, pattern library, quality standards.

  • System architecture diagrams
  • Data flow visualizations
  • Dependency mapping
  • Reconciliation-driven investigation
🔍

diagram-reviewer

model_role: critique

Independent quality review with structured PASS / WARN / FAIL verdicts.

  • Multi-dimensional quality scoring
  • Reconciliation value assessment
  • Actionable improvement recommendations
  • Author-reviewer separation of concerns
What’s Inside
Four Operations, One Tool

dot_validate

Three-layer validation pipeline:

  • Layer 1: Syntax — pydot parser catches malformed DOT
  • Layer 2: Structural — node/edge analysis, complexity
  • Layer 3: Render quality — graphviz confirms output

Layers 1–2 work without graphviz installed.

dot_render

SVG, PNG, PDF, or JSON output with any Graphviz layout engine. Six engines: dot, neato, fdp, circo, twopi, sfdp. Graceful degradation when graphviz is unavailable.

dot_analyze

8 NetworkX-powered graph analysis operations. Pure Python, zero LLM cost, millisecond execution. Stats, reachability, cycles, paths, critical path, diff, and more.

dot_setup

Environment detection with platform-specific install guidance. Checks what’s available, tells you exactly what to install for full capabilities.

What’s Inside
Skills & Knowledge Architecture

5 Discoverable Skills

dot-syntax

Complete DOT language reference

dot-patterns

Copy-paste templates for common diagrams

dot-as-analysis

Reconciliation-driven investigation process

dot-quality

Quality standards and review discipline

dot-graph-intelligence

Programmatic analysis methodology

Context Sink Pattern

Thin Awareness Context

Lightweight context gives every agent basic DOT awareness. Minimal token cost.

Mid-Weight Instructions

Agent-specific instructions load only when DOT work is delegated. Full methodology and patterns.

Heavy Docs on Demand

5 comprehensive doc files load only in agent sub-sessions via skill discovery. Never bloats parent context.

Architecture
Three Tiers — All Shipped

Tier 1: Knowledge

Agents, skills, docs, context.
Zero dependencies.

Tier 2: Validation

pydot-based. Graphviz optional.
Graceful degradation.

Tier 3: Intelligence

NetworkX, 8 operations.
Code-speed analysis.
